data.name: Steady State Expected (5x half-life of 2 days)
SRC Safety Data Review Through Day 23
15 mg Steady State Predose PK Sampling
Overnight Fast Before Dosing in CRU
Fasting Before Serum Chemistry at Screening/Check-in
SRC Safety Review Before Dose Titration
data.description: Safety Review Committee reviews available safety data through Day 23 for each participant before titration to 30 mg/day
data.text: Before titration to 30 mg/day, the Safety Review Committee (SRC) will review available safety data through Day 23 for each participant.

data.name: ALT Elevation Dose Modification Criteria
data.description: ALT greater than 5x ULN or greater than 3x ULN with bilirubin greater than 2x ULN triggers dose modification
data.text: ALT > 5x ULN requires temporary interruption with weekly repeat testing. ALT > 3x ULN with bilirubin > 2x ULN indicative of liver injury requires temporary interruption.

data.name: Hemoglobin Decrease Dose Modification Criteria
data.description: Hemoglobin decrease greater than 20% or greater than 30% from baseline triggers dose modification
data.text: > 20% decrease from baseline: reduce dose to previous level or 15 mg QOD. > 30% decrease from baseline: temporary interruption.

data.name: Platelet Decrease Dose Modification Criteria
data.description: Platelet decrease greater than 25% or greater than 30% from baseline triggers dose modification
data.text: > 25% decrease from baseline with platelets below reference range: reduce dose. > 30% decrease from baseline: temporary interruption.

data.name: Neutrophil Decrease Dose Modification Criteria
data.description: Neutrophil decrease greater than 30% from baseline triggers dose modification
data.text: > 30% decrease from baseline with neutrophils below reference range at baseline: reduce dose to previous dose level or 15 mg QOD.

data.name: Bilirubin Elevation with ALT Criteria
data.description: Bilirubin greater than 2x ULN accompanied by ALT greater than 3x ULN triggers temporary interruption
data.text: Bilirubin > 2x ULN accompanied by ALT > 3x ULN, indicative of liver injury: temporary interruption with weekly repeat testing.

data.name: Neurological Worsening Assessment
data.description: Evidence of neurologic worsening by AEs or neurologic physical exam triggers dose evaluation
data.text: Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor will evaluate the need for dose modification (interruption, increase or decrease) based on copper control parameters and relevant clinical data.

data.name: Psychiatric Worsening Assessment
data.description: Evidence of clinically significant acute psychiatric worsening triggers dose evaluation
data.text: Evidence of clinically significant acute psychiatric worsening which may include but not limited to suicidality, acute depression, or psychosis.

data.name: Safety Concern Preventing Dose Titration
data.description: Safety concerns that may place participants at undue risk prevent titration to 30 mg/day
data.text: Unless there are safety concerns that, in the opinion of the Investigator(s) and/or Alexion, may place participants at undue risk, in which case, participants may remain on the 15 mg/day dose for the duration of the study.

data.name: Maximum Rechallenge Limit
data.description: A maximum of 3 rechallenges will be allowed for dose modifications
data.text: A maximum of 3 rechallenges will be allowed.

data.name: QTcF Change From Baseline
data.description: Clinically significant change in QTcF from baseline after enrollment triggers evaluation
data.text: If a clinically significant finding is identified (including, but not limited to changes from baseline in QT interval corrected using Fridericia's formula [QTcF]) after enrollment, the Investigator or qualified designee will determine if the participant can continue in the study.

data.name: Outpatient Period Extension Approval
data.description: Investigator approval required for extending outpatient period up to 14 additional days
data.text: The Outpatient Period during Treatment Period 1 may be extended up to an additional 14 days with Investigator approval.

data.name: Screening to Enrollment Transition
data.text: Participants eligible for the study will be enrolled on Day -7 and initiated on a copper- and molybdenum-controlled diet. Enrolled means all participants who sign the ICF, are eligible for the study, and are registered on Day -7.

data.name: Run-in to Treatment Period 1 Transition
data.text: Following the Run-in Period, participants will be administered ALXN1840 at 15 mg/day starting Day 1. Participants must be off zinc therapy for minimum 21 days prior to Day 1 and off chelator therapy for approximately 4 days.

data.name: Inpatient Period 1 to Outpatient Period Transition
data.text: Participants may be discharged from the CRU on Day 9 to return on Day 22 or Day 23 (predose). At the CRU's discretion, participants may remain in the CRU during the outpatient period for safety or to maintain the integrity of the conduct of the study.

data.name: Outpatient Period to Inpatient Period 2 Transition
data.text: Participants will be re-admitted on Day 22 or Day 23 for Treatment Period 2 and will remain in house until Day 40. All procedures will start on Day 23.

data.name: 15 mg/day to 30 mg/day Dose Titration
data.text: Participants will be administered ALXN1840 at 15 mg/day on Day 1 through Day 28 and then increase to 30 mg/day on Day 29 through Day 39. Before titration to 30 mg/day, the SRC will review available safety data through Day 23 for each participant.

data.name: Treatment Period 2 to Follow-up Transition
data.text: Following completion of both inpatient periods of the study (ie, Day 40), participants will transition to prior therapy, other standard of care, or consider continuation of ALXN1840. All participants should return to the CRU for the EOS Visit on Day 54 (+/- 2 days).

data.name: Dose Reduction for ALT Elevation
data.text: ALT > 5x ULN: temporary interruption with weekly repeat testing. Rechallenge at 15 mg QOD or less frequent when ALT is below 3x ULN and trending toward baseline. Rechallenge requires approval of Alexion Medical Monitor.

data.name: Dose Reduction for Hemoglobin Decrease
data.text: > 20% decrease from baseline: reduce dose to previous dose level or 15 mg QOD, no further dose increase until resolution. > 30% decrease: temporary interruption with weekly repeat testing.

data.name: Dose Reduction for Platelet Decrease
data.text: > 25% decrease from baseline with platelets below reference range: reduce dose to previous level or 15 mg QOD. > 30% decrease: temporary interruption with weekly repeat testing.

data.name: Dose Reduction for Neutrophil Decrease
data.text: > 30% decrease from baseline with neutrophils below reference range: reduce dose to previous dose level or 15 mg QOD. No further dose increase until resolution.

data.name: Dose Modification for Bilirubin Elevation
data.text: Bilirubin > 2x ULN accompanied by ALT > 3x ULN: temporary interruption with weekly repeat testing. Rechallenge at 15 mg QOD or less frequent when bilirubin is below ULN. Rechallenge requires approval of Alexion Medical Monitor.

data.name: Dose Modification for Neurological Worsening
data.text: Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor will evaluate the need for dose modification (interruption, increase or decrease) based on copper control parameters and relevant clinical data. Rationale for dosing decision must be documented and re-evaluated at the next study visit.

data.name: Dose Modification for Psychiatric Worsening
data.text: Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor will evaluate the need for dose modification (interruption, increase or decrease) based on copper control parameters and relevant clinical data. Rationale for dosing decision must be documented and re-evaluated at the next study visit.

data.name: Discontinuation for Serious Hypersensitivity Reaction
data.text: Participants must be considered for discontinuation from study intervention if serious hypersensitivity reaction occurs.

data.name: Discontinuation for Severe Uncontrolled Infection
data.text: Participants must be considered for discontinuation from study intervention if severe uncontrolled infection occurs.

data.name: Discontinuation for Use of Disallowed Medication
data.text: Participants must be considered for discontinuation from study intervention if use of disallowed medication occurs.

data.name: Discontinuation for Pregnancy
data.text: Participants must be considered for discontinuation from study intervention if pregnancy or planned pregnancy occurs.

data.name: Discontinuation for Abnormal Liver Function
data.text: Discontinuation of study intervention for abnormal liver function should be considered by the Investigator when a participant meets one of the conditions outlined in the Dose Modification criteria or if the Investigator believes it is in the best interest of the participant.

data.name: Participant Voluntary Withdrawal
data.description: A participant may withdraw from the study at any time at his/her own request.

data.name: Investigator-Initiated Withdrawal
data.description: Participant may be withdrawn at any time at the discretion of the Investigator for safety, behavioral, compliance, or administrative reasons.

data.name: Discontinuation for Serious Hypersensitivity
data.description: Serious hypersensitivity reaction requires consideration for discontinuation from study intervention.

data.name: Discontinuation for Severe Uncontrolled Infection
data.description: Severe uncontrolled infection requires consideration for discontinuation from study intervention.

data.name: Discontinuation for Disallowed Medication Use
data.description: Use of disallowed medication requires consideration for discontinuation from study intervention.

data.name: Discontinuation for Pregnancy
data.description: Pregnancy or planned pregnancy requires consideration for discontinuation from study intervention.

data.name: Alexion or Investigator Decision to Discontinue
data.description: Alexion or the Investigator deems it is necessary for the participant to discontinue.

data.name: Lost to Follow-up
data.description: Participant repeatedly fails to return for scheduled visits and is unable to be contacted by the study site after 3 telephone calls and a certified letter. Participant will be considered to have withdrawn from the study.

data.name: Study Completion
data.description: A participant is considered to have completed the study if he/she has completed all phases of the study including the last scheduled procedure shown in the Schedule of Activities (EOS Visit on Day 54 +/- 2 days).

data.name: Study Termination by Alexion
data.description: Alexion reserves the right to close the study site or terminate the study at any time for any reason at the sole discretion of Alexion.

data.name: SRC Safety Review for Dose Titration Decision
data.description: Safety Review Committee reviews available safety data through Day 23 for each participant before titration from 15 mg/day to 30 mg/day on Day 29.

data.name: CRU Discharge vs Retention Decision on Day 9
data.description: Decision whether to discharge participant from CRU on Day 9 or retain for safety or study integrity. Outpatient period may be extended up to 14 additional days with Investigator approval.

data.name: ALT-based Dose Modification Decision
data.description: Ongoing monitoring decision point: if ALT > 5x ULN, temporary interruption required. If ALT > 3x ULN with bilirubin > 2x ULN, temporary interruption required. Rechallenge requires Medical Monitor approval.

data.name: Hematological Dose Modification Decision
data.description: Ongoing monitoring decision point: hemoglobin, platelet, or neutrophil decreases beyond thresholds trigger dose reduction or temporary interruption.

data.name: Neurological/Psychiatric Dose Modification Decision
data.description: Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor evaluate need for dose modification based on neurological or psychiatric worsening, copper control parameters, and relevant clinical data.

data.name: QTcF Clinical Significance Decision
data.description: If clinically significant QTcF change from baseline is identified after enrollment, Investigator determines if participant can continue and if any change in management is needed.

data.name: Post-Study Therapy Decision
data.description: Following completion of both inpatient periods (Day 40), participants will transition to prior therapy, other standard of care, or consider continuation of ALXN1840 under preapproval access at treating physician's discretion.
